conditions.message: The verifier provenance/policy implementation centralizes policy, kind, response normalization, and template plumbing instead of parallel per-call policy logic.
evidence: BrightOwl verified exactly the 10 packet paths are staged, stat is 671 insertions and 61 deletions, and git diff --cached --check produced no diagnostics.

internal/attestations/attestations.go centralizes VerifierKind, VerifierPolicy, NormalizeVerifierPolicy, EffectiveVerifierKind, VerifierPolicyAllows, ValidVerifierPolicy, ValidVerifierKind, and VerifierPolicyRecovery.

internal/backpressure/artifacts.go centralizes response validation, artifact building, blocked-response normalization, responseTemplateVerifier, responseVerifier, and normalizeConditionPolicy around shared attestation helpers.
internal/backpressure/prompts.go sets VerifierPolicy with normalizeConditionPolicy rather than duplicating policy values.
WhiteGorge independently returned pass for dry with matching evidence that the policy/provenance helpers and response-template logic are not duplicated.
Targeted tests and go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged policy model blocks the main reward-hacking path: main-agent evidence cannot satisfy default independent_required cells unless the manifest explicitly opts into main_agent_allowed.
evidence: BrightOwl verified NormalizeVerifierPolicy defaults empty policy to independent_required.
BrightOwl verified VerifierPolicyAllows accepts independent_required only for independent_subagent, while main_agent_allowed explicitly allows main_agent or independent_subagent.
validateResponseCondition rejects verifier kinds that are not allowed by the normalized condition policy before a response can pass.
normalizeBlockedCondition turns unacceptable verifier evidence into unknown with recovery text instead of preserving a fake pass.
TestValidatePassingVerifierPolicyKinds covers main agent blocked by independent default, missing verifier metadata, unknown verifier kind, and invalid verifier kind cases.

TestRunPreCommitAcceptsMainAgentWhenManifestAllows and TestRunPreCommitBlocksMainAgentUnderIndependentPolicy prove explicit main_agent_allowed passes while default independent_required blocks main-agent evidence.
WhiteGorge independently returned pass for anti-reward-hacking and confirmed the response template emits all 13 cells as independent_required with verifier.kind unknown placeholders.
Targeted tests and go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The governed verifier-policy/model behavior and response-template path have direct tests, including the risky policy-downgrade cases.
evidence: BrightOwl identified staged tests: TestValidatePassingVerifierPolicyKinds, TestRunPreCommitAcceptsMainAgentWhenManifestAllows, TestRunPreCommitBlocksMainAgentUnderIndependentPolicy, TestBuildResponsesTemplateIncludesEveryCondition, TestLoadManifestRejectsInvalidVerifierPolicy, and TestCommitResponsesTemplateEmitsCurrentMatrix.
The attestation model test covers independent, main-agent, CI, human, optional, none, unknown, and invalid verifier kinds.
The backpressure artifacts tests cover explicit main_agent_allowed and default independent_required blocking.
The CLI response-template test decodes the template and asserts current matrix cells, independent_required verifier_policy, and verifier placeholders.
The core test rejects invalid verifier_policy values while loading manifests.
WhiteGorge independently returned pass for one-function-one-test with matching test-function evidence.
Focused package tests passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload carries a governed data-integrity surface and satisfies it: verifier policy/provenance is normalized, validated, preserved into artifacts, and covered by focused tests for allowed and rejected cases.
evidence: ScarletMarsh read backpressure/data-integrity.md and verified the condition scope.

attestations validation now calls validateVerifierPolicyForPassing, blocked validation rejects pass with unacceptable verifier evidence, common validation checks valid verifier policy/kind, and policy helper functions define allowed verifier kinds.

LoadResponses now requires a top-level conditions array; BuildArtifact writes VerifierPolicy and Verifier to artifact cells; blocked normalization preserves verifier policy and sets policy-aware recovery text.
core.go validates condition verifier_policy while loading the manifest, preventing invalid policy strings from entering the plan.
attestations tests cover independent, legacy, main_agent_allowed, ci_allowed, human_allowed, optional none, optional unknown blocked, missing verifier metadata blocked, and invalid kind rejected.
backpressure artifacts tests cover main_agent_allowed acceptance, independent policy blocking main-agent evidence, top-level conditions validation, and response-template provenance placeholders.
Focused tests and go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload carries a governed security surface because it models verifier identity/provenance and evidence acceptance. The inspected code preserves the fabrication boundary: unacceptable verifier kinds/policies block, default policy stays independent_required, and the response template uses unknown placeholders rather than self-certifying evidence.
evidence: WhiteGorge verified verifier provenance records kind, agent, model, runtime, separate_context, transcript_ref, evidence_ref, and created_at on condition cells.
validateCommon rejects invalid verifier_policy and invalid verifier kind; ValidatePassing calls validateVerifierPolicyForPassing.
artifacts response validation enforces VerifierPolicyAllows before accepting responses, normalizes unacceptable evidence to unknown in blocked reports, and records responseVerifier into artifacts.
docs/attestation-schema.md documents default independent_required and states main_agent, ci, human, none, or unknown are not enough under the default policy.
WhiteGorge found no staged credential material with a secret/token/password/private-key scan.
WhiteGorge found no new file deletion, subprocess, network, env-dump, or secret-handling surfaces in the staged main policy files; matches were policy/schema strings and VerifierPolicyAllows calls.
BrightOwl independently returned pass for security-boundaries under A14b and matched the fabrication-boundary evidence.

conditions.message: The staged payload satisfies the autonomy-boundary surface: it does not let standing/init-time authorization count as per-cell evidence, and it requires verifier.kind to satisfy the manifest policy before a passing artifact is accepted.
evidence: ScarletMarsh read backpressure/autonomy-boundary.md and verified the condition scope.

independent_required only allows verifier.kind independent_subagent; main_agent_allowed, ci_allowed, human_allowed, and optional have explicit limited allowances, and unknown is not accepted for optional passing artifacts.

responseTemplateVerifier emits verifier.kind unknown placeholders instead of fabricating subagent confirmation; responseVerifier maps legacy subagent_confirmed true to independent_subagent but otherwise defaults to verifier.kind none.
docs/attestation-schema.md documents that under independent_required, main_agent, ci, human, none, or unknown is not enough, and missing or unacceptable verifier evidence must be explained.
TestRunPreCommitBlocksMainAgentUnderIndependentPolicy and TestRunPreCommitAcceptsMainAgentWhenManifestAllows prove standing/main-agent evidence is blocked unless the manifest explicitly allows it.
The /tmp/land07-verifier-prompts.json packet for autonomy-boundary exists and carries verifier_policy independent_required.
